title: EA Internal: Precautionary Flood Warning – River Sow at Stafford
evidence: Environment Agency issues precautionary flood warning for the River Sow at Stafford. Gauge data showing rapid rise. EA operational teams placed on elevated monitoring. Warning published to regional service.

assessment.justification: EA flood warning for the Severn at Shrewsbury is a credible early signal. Gauge rise at Welsh Bridge is consistent with observed rainfall in the upper catchment. Precautionary at this stage but significant for the local area.
assessment.synthesis: EA precautionary flood warning for the River Severn at Shrewsbury. First EA signal of the event for Shropshire. Upstream indicator of developing conditions.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Precautionary upstream warning. Conditions developing.

title: EA Internal: Flood Warning – River Arrow at Redditch
evidence: Environment Agency activates flood warning for the River Arrow through Redditch following reports of overtopping. EA field team deployed. Gauge data showing extreme flow rates from the upper catchment. Corroborates reports of rising levels.

assessment.justification: EA flood warning for Worcester area corroborates local highways reports. EA gauge data showing extreme flow rates adds hydrological authority to the event assessment. Field team deployment confirms severity level.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood warning active for Worcester city reaches. Gauge data confirms extreme flow. Strongly corroborates local disruption and adds hydrological evidence to the Worcestershire impact.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Active EA flood warning with gauge data and field team deployment.

title: EA Internal: Flood Warning Upgraded – River Chelt at Cheltenham
evidence: Environment Agency upgrades flood alert to flood warning for the River Chelt through Cheltenham. Gauge data showing rapid rise. EA officers attending property at risk. Notification sent to local properties.

assessment.justification: Flood warning upgrade at Hereford with gauge at 4.5m and rising. EA officer presence at at-risk properties confirms on-the-ground severity assessment. Direct notification to properties adds community dimension.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood warning upgraded at Hereford with gauge at 4.5m and rising. EA officers attending. Strongly corroborates National Highways A40 advisory and local social media reports for Hereford.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Authoritative EA flood warning upgrade with gauge data and officer attendance on the ground.

title: EA Internal: Flood Warning – River Yeo at Yeovil
evidence: Environment Agency activates flood warning for the River Yeo at Yeovil as gauge reaches critical levels and continues to rise. EA staff deployed along the floodplain. Warning covers residential properties in the low-lying sections. Predicted peak level expected late afternoon.

assessment.justification: EA flood warning at Bridgwater with gauge at 3.2m and rising. Predicted peak represents a significant flood event for the Parrett floodplain. EA staff deployment and predictive modelling adds authoritative assessment.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood warning active at Bridgwater with River Parrett rising toward predicted peak. EA staff deployed. Hydrological peak expected by late afternoon. Corroborates all concurrent official and community reporting from Somerset.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Active EA flood warning with gauge data, staff deployed, and hydrological peak prediction. Extensively corroborated.

title: EA Internal: Flood Warning – River Frome at Stroud
evidence: Environment Agency issues flood warning for the River Frome at Stroud. Gauge data showing levels well above normal. EA field officers on site monitoring key culverts. Flood warning notification sent to registered properties in the Stroud valley.

assessment.justification: EA flood warning at Ross-on-Wye is authoritative confirmation of the River Wye conditions reported by National Highways and the Hereford Times. Gauge at 4.2m is well above normal and rising trajectory is concerning for A40 and adjacent properties.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood warning at Ross-on-Wye confirms River Wye at 4.2m and rising. Corroborates National Highways A40 advisory and regional media reporting. 120 properties notified.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Official EA flood warning with gauge data. Cross-corroborated by National Highways, local media, and 120 properties notified.

title: EA Internal: Flood Alert Issued – River Severn at Gloucester
evidence: Environment Agency issues a flood alert for the River Severn at Gloucester. Levels rising slowly. Alert is precautionary given the substantial volumes of water moving through the catchment from the English Midlands and the Marches. Monitoring continues.

assessment.justification: EA flood alert for Gloucester Severn is a downstream propagation signal. The Severn receives flow from several upstream catchments already in flood. Precautionary at present but hydrologically consistent.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood alert for the Severn at Gloucester. Precautionary but hydrologically expected given the scale of the upstream events. Downstream propagation signal. Corroborates Gloucestershire as a secondary impact area.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Precautionary downstream alert. Hydrologically consistent with upstream events.
